barks.text: One sentence. Grandpa could do it in five words. I usually need nine and a comma I regret.
barks.speaker: gary

barks.text: Home. The board's empty and the kettle's patient. Enjoy that, Gibbons — empty boards don't stay empty in this business.
barks.speaker: gary

barks.text: The Percolator, mid-morning. If Lanternside has a heartbeat, it's the espresso machine. Steady. A little dramatic on the steam wand.
barks.speaker: gary

barks.text: Dot's already three paragraphs deep in someone else's mistake. Best not.
barks.speaker: gary

barks.text: Market Row before a festival: string lights going up, prices going down, and Gino conducting the whole street like an orchestra that owes him money.
barks.speaker: gary

barks.text: The monument's been scrubbed so hard it looks embarrassed. Fifty years tonight. Whole district's holding its breath, and the bunting's holding most of it.
barks.speaker: gary

barks.text: The square empties slow after bad news, like nobody wants to be the first to admit it's real. Somebody knew that vault would be empty. Somebody has known for days.
barks.speaker: gary

barks.text: That's enough thread for one day. Time to see what it ties to.
barks.speaker: gary

barks.text: Four days chasing everyone else's loss. Grandpa's package is still in that dark somewhere too. …Working on it, old man.
barks.speaker: gary

barks.text: Otto's mid-pour. Interrupting a pour is, in this establishment, a crime with witnesses.
barks.speaker: gary

barks.text: Margie's got a tray in each wing and a rumor on hold. Later.
barks.speaker: gary

barks.text: Gino is mid-aria about a melon. There is no safe way in.
barks.speaker: gary

barks.text: Ida's holding a lock mid-pick. House rules: you wait, or you lose the good conversation.
barks.speaker: gary

barks.text: Evelyn's shears are moving. A wise reporter respects the blade radius.
barks.speaker: gary

barks.text: Poppy is three clipboards deep and moving at hummingbird speed. Catching her now would require a net, and ethics.
barks.speaker: gary

barks.text: Warren's back with the pigeons. The speech must be close; the front row looks moved.
barks.speaker: gary

barks.text: Julian's shaking hands down a receiving line that keeps refilling. The smile hasn't moved in an hour. That takes training, or fear.
barks.speaker: gary

barks.text: Clara's reading on the bench with the posture of someone timing everyone who interrupts her. Not twice in one day.
barks.speaker: gary

barks.text: Milo's on a stakeout. He'd never forgive me for blowing his cover, which is a cardboard box labeled BOX.
barks.speaker: gary

barks.text: Tuck has achieved perfect stillness against that lamppost. Waking him would be a crime, and he'd know.
barks.speaker: gary

barks.text: Her glasses fog when the key comes up. Every time. Like weather.
barks.speaker: gary

barks.text: He's cleaned those glasses three times since I said the boy's name. They were clean the first time.
barks.speaker: gary

barks.text: The ring again. Around, around. His father's, too loose, and he spins it exactly when the future comes up.
barks.speaker: gary
